title: Randomized Algorithms, Approximation, and Honest Guarantees · Counterexample Repair Bay
summary: Choose scalable methods when exact optimization is too expensive or adversarial. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a probabilistic and approximation guarantee report and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish Las Vegas and Monte Carlo guarantees, failure probability, and expected cost.
Analyze random pivot selection against input-order adversaries and seeded tests.
Reduce independent error probability through repeated trials and justified aggregation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
